surface.name: Better Stack Uptime first HTTP monitor
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, Uptime is the fastest commonly used documented route among peer product surfaces: the official guide explicitly names `Creating your first monitor` and ends with a running HTTP monitor.

alternatives_considered.name: Tracing
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a separate product surface with application instrumentation and language-specific setup.

alternatives_considered.name: Log management
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This requires selecting and configuring a log source rather than following the named first-monitor path.

alternatives_considered.name: Infrastructure monitoring
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This requires host or cluster instrumentation and is not the selected first-monitor guide.

alternatives_considered.name: Incident management and on-call
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a peer product surface for alert routing and incident response, not a universal first-use route.

alternatives_considered.name: Status pages
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a peer communication surface whose setup and terminal differ from a running monitor.

alternatives_considered.name: Error tracking and real user monitoring
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a peer application-instrumentation surface with framework-specific setup.

alternatives_considered.name: AI SRE
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a peer investigation and incident-response surface, not the named first-monitor task.

entry_point.developer_intent: Start without a Better Stack account, create the first HTTP status monitor for a URL, retain the basic single-user escalation behavior where applicable, and verify that the monitor is running and continuously checking the URL.
assumed_prior_state: No Better Stack account, team membership, monitor, alert channel, escalation policy, or prior configuration is assumed.
A browser, email address, reachable URL or IP address, and network connection are available.
No email was submitted, magic link was requested or opened, account or team was created or joined, or monitor was created during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Create the first HTTP status monitor.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A Better Stack Uptime monitor is running and continuously checking the supplied URL or IP address for an HTTP 2XX success response.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: After Create monitor is clicked, the official guide states that the monitor is up and running and continuously checking the URL.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The guide states `The monitor is now up and running, continuously checking the URL.`

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The first running monitor is the earliest explicit functioning Uptime result; bulk imports and advanced monitors are next steps.

prerequisites.requirement: Provide an email address and receive a Better Stack magic link.
Join an existing team associated with the email domain or create a new account when that branch is offered.
Have a URL or IP address that Better Stack can check for an HTTP 2XX response.

primary_path.action: Open Better Stack's `Creating your first monitor` guide.
primary_path.output: The four-step monitor route is visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The HTTP status monitor instructions open.

primary_path.action: Sign up for Better Stack at the official signup URL and enter Uptime.
details: Treat signup, magic-link verification, and initial team creation as one opaque prerequisite under the workshop policy.
primary_path.output: An authenticated Better Stack team is available.
primary_path.success_signal: The Uptime Monitors page can be opened.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A domain match can route the user to an existing team.

primary_path.action: Go to Monitors, select Create monitor, and enter a reachable URL or IP address.
primary_path.input: A URL expected to return HTTP 2XX
primary_path.success_signal: The target appears in the URL to monitor field.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A non-2XX result creates an incident after monitoring begins.

details: Documented choices include phone, SMS, email, mobile push, Slack, and Microsoft Teams.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Some channels require their own integration setup.

primary_path.action: For a single-user team, leave the default escalation option selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Multi-user teams can require an on-call schedule and escalation policy.

primary_path.success_signal: The monitor is up and running and continuously checking the URL.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Investigate target reachability if checks do not return 2XX.

branches.condition: The intended first value belongs to a Better Stack surface other than Uptime
branches.path: Choose that product guide instead of the Uptime first-monitor candidate.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes prerequisites, actions, and the terminal outcome.

branches.condition: An existing team is associated with the submitted email domain
branches.path: Join that team or choose Sign up for a new account instead.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the account and team context before the same first-monitor form.

branches.condition: The user is on a single-user team
branches.path: Leave the default escalation option selected.

branches.condition: Multiple users or teams use Uptime
branches.path: Explore on-call scheduling and alerting and configure an escalation policy appropriate to the team.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds organization-specific alert routing choices before monitor creation.

branches.condition: A different alert delivery channel is required
branches.path: Choose phone, SMS, email, mobile push, Slack, Microsoft Teams, or another documented integration.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes alert-channel setup while preserving the same running-monitor terminal.

friction_gates.description: The documentation home presents multiple peer product surfaces without selecting one universal first value outcome.
A Better Stack account or existing-team membership is required before the monitor form can be used.
Signup uses an email magic link, but its post-submit and verification states are not publicly documented.
A domain match can introduce an existing-team versus new-account choice.
The monitor requires a URL or IP address and an alerting choice.
Escalation setup differs for single-user and multi-user teams.

sources.title: Get started with monitoring | Better Stack Documentation
sections_used: Step 1: Choosing the URL to monitor

uncertainties.question: Which Better Stack product surface should define platform-wide first success?
uncertainties.impact: The prerequisites, route, and completion signal differ across Uptime, incidents and on-call, status pages, infrastructure monitoring, logs, tracing, error tracking, and AI SRE.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official documentation home presents these as peer surfaces and does not select one universal first task.

uncertainties.question: What exact states follow Send me a magic link for a new user?
uncertainties.impact: The mandatory signup and product-entry segment cannot be recorded atomically.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The public signup page shows the initial controls but the official docs do not enumerate delivery, verification, profile, team, and Uptime convergence states.

uncertainties.question: Which alert method is preselected for a new single-user account?
uncertainties.impact: The exact monitor-form click path may vary.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The guide lists alerting options and a default escalation behavior but does not identify a universal alert-channel default.

excluded_after_success: Bulk importing monitors, adding advanced monitor types, configuring status pages, instrumenting traces or logs, and broader incident-management work occur after the first monitor is running.
